text:
PREVENTION

FATIGUE

WHAT ARE THE
MAIN CAUSES
OF ANEMIA?

Anemia is very common in IBD, especially during the first
year after diagnosis (about 2/3 of patients) and its causes are numerous:
= Acute bleeding (rapid drop in hemoglobin) or chronic (progressive drop in hemoglobin due to chronic iron loss) during flares or in poorly controlled disease (so-called chronic active forms)

#O7

AND DEFICIENCIES

WHAT IS A DEFICIENCY?

A deficiency is the lack of an important element
for the proper functioning of the body. We
will address deficiencies in vitamins, iron and trace
elements. Fatigue, but also shortness of breath (particularly
with exertion) or a faster heart rate are clearly among the classic symptoms of anemia,
whereas there is no clear scientific proof that a vitamin
deficiency alone is a sufficient cause of fatigue. One can nevertheless think pragmatically
that it is better not to have deficiencies in order to feel well.

WHAT IS ANEMIA?
